results.content:
How to design your email

## Your questions, answered

 Canva Email makes it simple to create engaging emails using our drag-and-drop editor, no coding or complex email tools needed. You get access to hundreds of professionally designed email templates, rich visual content, AI-powered tools, and HTML export. Plus, you can create all your marketing materials on Canva, making it easy to stay on-brand and removing the hassle of switching between multiple apps. [...] Canva Email can be used for all types of emails, whether you’re a small business owner, a marketing specialist, or a content creator. Use it to create professional marketing newsletters, campaign announcements, client onboarding emails, or internal newsletters. You can also use it for creative event invitations, exciting product launch announcements, or personal projects.

 Yes! Canva Email is free to use. However, some features and elements may require a Pro account or the purchase of Canva credits.

 Once you export your email design as an HTML file, you can use it on platforms such as Gmail, Outlook, Mailchimp, Hubspot, and any other apps that let you send emails. [...] ### Magic Write™ for email

  Create email copies with Magic Write™, an AI writer & text generator powered by OpenAI.
 Canva AI

  ### Canva AI

  Tag @Canva in comments to apply updates or get suggestions to polish your email.
 Magic Resize

  ### Magic Resize

  Transform your existing marketing content into on-brand emails with Magic Resize.

## How to design your email

1. Launch Canva

   Click “Email” on the homepage. You can also click “Create” at the upper left-hand corner and type “Email” in the search bar.
2. Choose a template

   Pick an email design template that fits your needs, like a marketing or a newsletter email design template. Or start with a blank page.
3. Customize your email design

results.title: 12 Best Free HTML Email Template Builders & Editors
results.content:
The business license has all unlocked blocks, you can choose from 900+ Google Fonts, export to any email software you like. A license is $15 / month. Designmodo’s Postcards works with predesigned blocks and elements that have some customization options. As you can’t make your own blocks, gives less flexibility than other editors. Upside is that working with Postcards becomes more straight forward that way.

## 12. Topol.io

“Beautiful emails, made easy”

Topol is an email builder tool we chose to include in this list because of how easy it is to use. In Topol PRO you can choose one of their free email templates to try. You can also design a brand new template from your own HTML or scratch. [...] ## 6. MailChimp’s Email Editor

Mailchimp is the most famous email marketing automation platform in the world. Founded in 2001 by Ben Chestnut, Mark Armstrong, and Dan Kurzius. There are a lot of Mailchimp Alternatives today, like Brevo because of their popularity.

Mailchimp is an ESP, however, they have a built-in email template builder.

Although it’s not very flexible in terms of elements and blocks, Mailchimp allows users to create nice responsive emails.

Mailchimp mainly targets small and medium businesses that want to grow. You can either select a precoded email template or create an email template from scratch. There are plenty of cases when a custom email template is best, but in the free version of MailChimp it is no longer possible to import your own HTML. [...] It is best as an alternative if you have basic requirements and are looking for an easy and fast way to create a simple template, without thinking too much about integration and compatibility features.

There are many other email editors built into other email marketing tools, but in a few cases, you cannot export the HTML code from them, or the email code is a question mark – so you have to test each and every mail to make sure it is compatible.

The downside of using the ESP’s built-in email editor is that you are locked to that service. The upside is that you don’t need to go to another provider to deal with email design.

#### 5. TOPOL

“Beautiful emails, made easy”

TOPOL SampleSource: TOPOL

TOPOL is very similar to Unlayer. It offers a simple, drag-and-drop visual editor, pre-made templates, and reusable blocks. The difference between these two platforms is that TOPOL offers a paid version. (Very affordable, but still!) 

Their paid version includes support for dynamic content. You also get unlimited test emails with this plan. And again, like Unlayer you have the option to embed this tool as a plug-in for your existing platforms. 

At-a-glance features:

★ feature: Support for dynamic content (paid)

 A no-code, drag-and-drop visual editor
 Support for exporting HTML email templates
 Reusable content blocks [...] ##### Jeni McGuigan, Email Designer at AWeber

#### Q: What should you look for in an email builder?

There are three main things your email builder should excel at: Simplicity, consistency, and integration.

Simplicity: An email builder should be easy enough for anyone to use the first time they try (not just coders). 

Consistency: It may seem obvious, but the email you build should look the same for anyone who opens it — in Gmail, Yahoo, Apple Mail, Outlook, or any other platform. Find an email builder that takes care of this for you.

Integration: Email builders should be able to connect to other services you love to use. For example, designing with Canva inside your email builder (like AWeber or Dyspatch) can make the process of creating beautiful emails easy and fun. [...] You’re a small-scale contractor creating email templates for clients.
 You’re a freelancer who's just looking to create the occasional HTML email.
 You run a small business and want to keep your email marketing costs low.
 You’re part of a small team and want to start improving your email workflow in preparation for growth.
 You want to experiment with an email builder before committing to a paid solution.

### What are the best options in this category?

#### 1. BEE Free

“Create beautiful emails and landing pages, fast”

BEEFree SampleSource: BEE Free

BEE Free is my favorite non-paid option. Their product is simple and functional. Any non-technical user can hop in and create a responsive email from scratch or by leveraging one of their pre-made templates.

results.title: 9 Best Email Template Builders and Editors
results.content:
### Best Responsive Email Template Builder

All the email template builders on this list come with responsive templates, so it really depends on whether you can find a design that you like within one of their libraries. Stripo and Beefree have the largest collection of templates to choose from. If you are still interested in finding a Stripo alternative, please check out our top alternatives here.

Though the templates offered by these email design tools will have already been tested, it’s important to do your own spam and design testing once you’ve finalized your email to make sure there are no problems with email deliverability or display. Tools such as GlockApps or Litmus can help with this. Or, your email service provider might also have built-in spam and design testing. [...] The Basic plan will cost you $20/month and gives you 50 stored templates per project, 50 exports to your email service provider per month, one collaborator, and the ability to test your template on 2 email clients.

The Medium plan will cost you $45/month and gives you 300 stored templates per project, 300 exports to your email service provider per month and the ability to test your template on 2 email clients. Up to 3 users.

The Pro plan will cost you $95/month and gives you unlimited stored templates per project, unlimited exports to your email service provider and the ability to test your template on 3 email clients. Up to 10 users.

Try Stripo email for free!

Check out these Stripo alternatives

### 2. Tabular (Dedicated email template builder)

results.title: 12 Best Email Template Builders For 2026 [Free & Paid]
results.content:
Best feature: Advanced image editing options

Chamaileon deserves a place on our email template builder list due to its user-friendly email designer and powerful image editing capabilities.

This email template creator offers a free stock photo library from Pexels, but you can easily upload your own email images and customize them to your liking, adding filters and more.

When it comes to templates, Chamaileon has 135+ beautiful email designs you can use, which are also mobile responsive. The clean interface lets you focus on email template creation while the re-usable email blocks make the process even easier. [...] What’s more, there are over 400 pre-designed templates that are easy to customize by adding design blocks like images, videos, or GIFs. Topol.io also lets you use high-quality, royalty-free images from Pexels to enhance the appearance of your emails.

This email template builder supports collaboration, allowing marketers to share templates with their team and work together to refine the design. Last but not least, you can incorporate your products directly into your email campaigns via XML feeds.

TL;DR: Topol.io is a simple tool that’s efficient at creating beautiful email templates. It also comes at a competitive price. Despite that, its interface feels somewhat “basic” compared to the other tools in the list.

### Topol.io Features [...] It has a vast library of 1600+ templates, and its ease of use makes it a great tool to complement your email service provider. There’s a dedicated category for AMP emails with more than 90 ready-made designs to create dynamic emails.

Alternatively, you can ask the Stripo team to design a custom email template for you according to your brand guidelines or website.

The drag-and-drop editor allows you to create responsive templates that look good regardless of screen size. Besides the simpler blocks, you can incorporate an AMP Form block to collect subscriber feedback through your email.

Another feature that strikes out is Stripo’s personal library of custom modules that you can save and reuse to speed up template creation.
